rulings.Case Title: KAISER KASABIAN et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Service For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction And Set Aside The Requst For Entry Of Default
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 13, 2008, line 1. DEFENDANT CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY, INC'S Motion To Quash Service For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction And Set Aside The Request For Entry Of Default is denied. It appears that defendant and/or the entity it acquired, Mundet, had sufficient contacts with California for the Court to exercise jurisdiction and that plaintiff's claims relate to the contacts. Cassiar Mining Corp v. Superior Court, 66 Cal. App. 4th 550, 555. If there is any doubt about this, plaintiff is entitled to depose defendant's person most knowledgable and its custodian of records on the jurisdictional issue. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: GENE CHAN et al VS. UNITED SCREENERS ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 13, 2008, line 4. DEFENDANTS GEORGE VALDES, STEPHEN BURKE AND UNITED SCREENERS ASSOCIATION LOCAL ONE'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is denied as to the 1st cause of action. The Court cannot conclude as a matter of law that the statements were understood as hyberpolic jokes. Denied as to the 5th cause of action. The Court previously rejected this argument; the flyer can be viewed as a solicitation of services and C C section 3344 (d) is not applicable. Grant without leave to amend as to 2nd cause of action by plaintiffs against USA, Local 1 and Burke; and 3rd and 4th causes of action against defendants. These claims are duplicative of the defamation claim. Dinapoli is granted leave to amend. Grant without leave to amend as 6th cause of action. Dinapoli does not allege a relationship with plaintiff. Grant without leave to amend as to intentional infliction of emotional distress as duplicative of defamation claim. Dipanoli has leave to amend based on harrassing conduct. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: LES JANKEY et al VS. SONG KOO LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Atty Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 13, 2008, line 7. DEFENDANT SONG LEE'S Motion For Attorney Fees is continued to August 28, 2008. The parties are to submit a brief, not to exceed 10 pages, as to what is required to demonstrate that an action is "frivilous, unreasonable or without foundation," and what facts support or do not support defendant's contention that plaintiff learned that Health & Safety Code section 19955 claim was frivilous. The submission is to be filed on or before August 25, 2008. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY S. FRUCIANO VS. JOHN TOBONI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 13, 2008, line 10. PLAINTIFF ANTHONY FRUCIANO'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT is denied. Plaintiff's motion fails to comply with CCP section 663. Plaintiff is relying on new evidence not uncontroverted evidence that previously was before the Court. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY S. FRUCIANO VS. JOHN TOBONI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For New Trial
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 13, 2008, line 10. PLAINTIFF ANTHONY FRUCIANO'S Motion For New Trial is denied. The evidence cited is not material. This was a voluntary mediation. Cal. Rule of Court 3.855 is not applicable to private mediations. The Carbone Declaration demonstrates that no reasonable person would conclude that the prior lawsuit had any bearing on Judge Chiantelli being able to mediate the dispute. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: SELLERS' REPRESENTATIVE, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. IRELAND SAN FILIPPO, L.L.P., A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File X-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 13, 2008, line 11. DEFENDANT IRELAND SAN FILIPPO, LLP'S Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint is denied without prejudice because counsel's declaration fails to demonstrate why it would be in the interest of justice to grant leave and an explanation as to why it was not submitted at an earlier date. CCP 426.50; The Rutter Guide Civil Procedure Before Trial 6.562. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: 2710 BAKER STREET PARTNERS VS. EDWARD SAVIO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reasonable Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 13, 2008, line 18. DEFENDANTS EDWARD SAVIO AND NATALIE SAYLOR-SAVIO'S Motion For Reasonable Attorney Fees. Fees in the sum of $26,876.25 and costs of $600.00 are awarded. The Rent Board hearing was not pre-litigation activity. It was a distinct proceeding and was not based on enforcing a term of the lease. The hourly rate of $350.00 is reasonable and defendant has shown that $1015.00 of the requested fees are for unrelated matters and $11,200.00 relates to the Rent Board. =(302/PJM)
